Punjab farmers protest India-US trade deal, burn effigies of PM Modi, Trump
description: Farmers in Punjab protested the proposed India-US trade agreement, fearing it will harm domestic agriculture, labourers, and small traders. Burning effigies of leaders, they demanded the pact's cancellation, citing concerns over increased imports and unfair competition from subsidized American farmers. This opposition echoes past farm law debates, with calls for MSP guarantees and irrigation reforms.

title: Deepti Sharma on verge of history as India star eyes world record vs Bangladesh
description: Indian all-rounder Deepti Sharma is on the cusp of cricket history, needing just one wicket against Bangladesh to become the leading wicket-taker in women's international cricket, surpassing Jhulan Goswami.  Despite a recent loss, Deepti's consistent performance, especially her five-wicket haul, highlights her prowess.  However, Team India faces middle-order batting concerns and fielding lapses ahead of this crucial World Cup encounter.

title: ‘Man enough to apologise’: Ben Stokes takes responsibility after England axe
French mother, 5 children rescued from Pak home after decade-long captivity
description: A French mother and her five children have been dramatically rescued from a decade of alleged captivity and abuse in Pakistan's Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province. Sylvie Yasmina, who claims her husband 'effectively imprisoned' them after moving from Australia in 2014, was found with visible injuries. Her son's brave escape to a police station led to the family's liberation and her expressed desire to return to France.

Karnataka tourist slips from rocks, drowns off Goa coast
description: A tourist from Karnataka tragically drowned in North Goa after slipping from treacherous rocks near Baga-Arpora. Ashpaq Masli, 34, ignored monsoon warnings and ventured onto the slippery terrain for photos, a common but dangerous practice.  Witnesses saw him lose his footing and get swept away by the sea.  Authorities continue to urge caution despite installed signboards highlighting the risks.

Farmer's daughter Anushka Yadav scripts history, becomes India's youngest ...
description: Eighteen-year-old Anushka Yadav etched her name in history at the National Inter-State Championships, becoming the youngest Indian athletics national record holder. She shattered the women's hammer throw record with a remarkable 67.02m, surpassing the previous mark twice during the competition. Hailing from a farming family, Yadav's journey from rural Uttar Pradesh to national glory is an inspiring tale of dedication and talent.

When data met Donald – and died: How US Prez trumped India with fake tariff numbers
description: A new book reveals President Trump's disregard for factual trade data concerning India, often dismissing official figures as 'bullshit numbers' to justify his imposition of high tariffs.  Despite evidence from USTR and trade representatives, Trump insisted on inflated tariff rates, leading to strained US-India relations.  This approach extended to foreign policy, where his administration prioritized 'feelings over facts,' impacting global partnerships.

'Will leave only after killing you': How a water tap dispute turned fatal for Bengaluru PG owner
description: A paying guest owner in Bengaluru tragically died after being brutally assaulted by two students over a dispute involving a water tap. The youths returned hours after an initial confrontation, issuing death threats. The owner, attempting to defend his family, was overpowered and fatally attacked with a cricket bat. His son recounted his father's dying wish to file a complaint and ensure justice.

title: Another top US army officer abruptly steps down under war secy Hegseth's watch
description: General Christopher Donahue, the last American soldier to exit Afghanistan in 2021, is stepping down as commander of US Army Europe and Africa after just 18 months. His departure aligns with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's initiative to reduce senior military leadership. The Army is reportedly considering downgrading the command, a move that follows criticism of European allies.

title: GTA 6 Ultimate Edition pre-orders: Price, bonuses, release details and more
description: Pre-orders for Grand Theft Auto VI begin June 25, with the game launching November 19, 2026, on PS5 and Xbox Series X/S. Two editions, Standard ($79.99) and digital-only Ultimate ($99.99), are available. All pre-orders receive the Vintage Vice City Pack. No PC version is confirmed for launch, and physical copies will include a download code.

title: Suspended for Vijay’s Jana Nayagan leak, editor Pradeep E Raghav joins Rajinikanth’s Dharman
India's 2036 Olympic bid gets timeline: IOC reveals when the host will be finalised
description: India's 2036 Olympic hosting aspirations gained momentum as the IOC approved a new selection process, aiming for a host announcement by mid-2029.  This revamped system introduces a "Strategic Dialogue" phase for greater transparency and clarity.  Candidates, including India with Ahmedabad as the proposed city, will undergo rigorous evaluation, including financial guarantees and site visits, before a final decision is made.

Bill Gates says Jeffrey Epstein sought to blackmail him over extramarital affairs
description: Bill Gates revealed to US lawmakers that Jeffrey Epstein appeared to be contemplating blackmail over his extramarital affairs, citing "veiled" threats and draft emails. Gates, testifying before the House Oversight Committee, stated he was never blackmailed but acknowledged Epstein explored using personal information to maintain influence. He expressed regret over his association with the late sex offender, calling it a "grave error in judgment."

title: 'Not mentally ready, under family pressure to marry': Cops' big claim in Pune fiancee murder case
‘I had one week to live’: Muni Long reveals double lung transplant following Lupus
description: Muni Long, a Grammy-winning artist, bravely divulged her challenging health ordeal that culminated in a double lung transplant amidst a tour-related crisis. Faced with a grim prognosis of just one week to live, she confronted a profound dilemma: hospice care or surgery. Her wish to live for her son guided her choice, and the transplant surprisingly improved her singing voice, although she remains hesitant about performing.

title: ‘Merely asked to close door’: Son stabbed 4 times in Mumbai local, grieving father in disbelief

description: Michael Dell has surged into the world's top 5 richest individuals, surpassing Larry Ellison. This ascent is fueled by Dell Technologies' booming AI infrastructure business, which saw a remarkable 757% surge in AI server revenue.  While Dell's wealth has climbed significantly, Ellison's fortune has seen a dip, leading to this notable shift in global wealth rankings.

title: Urinary urgency in 40s: Why women shouldn’t ignore this common symptom
Can employees who never got appointment letters demand one now? What new labour code says
description: Employers must now issue appointment letters in a standardized format to all staff, including those in informal sectors, detailing job roles, pay, and benefits before work commences. This new mandate aims to prevent disputes and enhance clarity on service conditions. Existing employees also have three months to receive these letters. Non-compliance incurs significant financial penalties, with states expected to align their rules soon.

description: According to sources, legal and constitutional experts attached to Parliament are currently examining the matter and are expected to provide their recommendations before a final decision is taken. Previous rulings by presiding officers in comparable situations are also being studied to ensure any decision is legally and constitutionally sound. Apart from the rebel factions of the TMC and Shiv Sena (UBT), the DMK has also sought a separate seating arrangement following the collapse of its long-standing alliance with the Congress.

title: Rajkumar Hirani admits Dunki didn’t appeal to masses: ‘Middle class get their visas easily’

JJ Watt recalls dating struggles and his longtime crush on Jennifer Aniston
description: Former NFL star J.J. Watt once revealed his struggles with dating due to his high-profile career and the intense public scrutiny that could scare his to-be-girlfriend. He shared his desire for a partner who valued personality and could share simple joys. He emphasized mutual enhancement in a relationship. Interestingly, Watt also recounted a memorable encounter with his celebrity crush, Jennifer Aniston, describing it as a surreal, fan-like experience. He is currently happily married to Kealia Ohai, a former professional soccer player.

Josh Hokit stands by Michelle Obama remark despite White, Cormier backlash
description: Josh Hokit has defended his controversial Michelle Obama remark, saying he believed it was a compliment and an example of free speech. His explanation has not reduced criticism, with figures such as Dana White and Daniel Cormier strongly condemning the comment. Hokit, however, continues to stand by his words and has refused to apologize.

title: ‘Suffered a heatstroke’: Italy rebuts Nato chief's claim on US flights supporting Iran strikes
description: Italy has refuted Nato chief Mark Rutte's claim that hundreds of US aircraft flew from Italian bases for operations against Iran. Rome insists only technical and logistical support was authorized, not combat missions. This clarification follows Rutte's remarks to Fox News, which sparked political backlash in Italy. The government maintains its actions align with constitutional and treaty obligations, refusing requests beyond approved limits.

description: England's goalless World Cup draw with Ghana drew attention after Ghanaian witch doctor Nana Kwaku Bonsam claimed he had cursed Harry Kane and later lifted the hex following the striker's missed chance. The bizarre story sparked headlines, while pundits criticised England's flat performance and said they were fortunate to avoid defeat. Despite concerns, most believe Thomas Tuchel's side remains well placed ahead of its final group-stage match against Panama.

Xi's bold move: China to enforce its new ethnic unity law, globally
description: The Ethnic Unity and Progress Promotion Law, which comes into force on July 1, seeks to strengthen what Beijing describes as a shared national identity among the country's ethnic groups. One provision extends potential legal liability to people and organisations outside China, an aspect that has drawn particular scrutiny. Rights advocates warn that the measure could provide fresh legal grounds for targeting overseas campaigners from communities such as the Uyghurs and Tibetans.

'You need to apologise': Ben Stokes breaks silence on nightclub controversy
description: England captain Ben Stokes said he apologised to teammates after he and Gus Atkinson missed the second Test for breaching the team's midnight curfew. Stokes admitted the nightclub controversy overshadowed the debuts of several young players and affected the squad. Cleared of serious misconduct but issued warnings, Stokes stressed accountability, denied any rift with coach Brendon McCullum, and said the team is now fully focused on winning the series decider against New Zealand.

'Suvendu supported me a lot': Mahua recalls 'very good personal ties' with Bengal CM
description: "In 2014, I was supposed to get a Lok Sabha ticket, but it didn't happen, and I cried all night. It was Suvendu who supported me. Later, he joined the BJP, and we don't speak anymore because he is in a different political party. But no one can forget our personal relationship, " Mahua Moitra said.

title: Mountaineering body appeals for safety during monsoon trekking in Sahyadris
Hot country, cold shoulder: Pew survey shows Trump's global approval tanking
description: Global opinion on Donald Trump is overwhelmingly negative, with a median of just 23% expressing confidence in his leadership. A new Pew survey reveals widespread disapproval of his handling of foreign policy, including Iran and trade tariffs. Confidence in the US as a reliable partner has plummeted, particularly in Canada and Europe, painting a stark contrast to Trump's claims of global admiration.

Sooryavanshi to get a separate changing room in England: ECB reveals why
description: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, recently called up to India’s national team, could become the country’s youngest international cricketer if he debuts against Ireland. During the England tour, he will use a separate changing room under ICC and ECB safeguarding rules for under-16 players. The ECB and BCCI are coordinating arrangements, while his parents are expected to accompany him throughout the tour.

Japan’s second richest man SoftBank’s Masayoshi Son junks his plan to retire in sixties
description: SoftBank's Masayoshi Son, 68, plans to lead the tech giant for another decade, focusing intensely on artificial intelligence and robotics.  Dismissing 'AI bubble' fears as 'blasphemy,' Son believes AI's potential is just unfolding.  He aims to achieve 'artificial superintelligence' and is backing this vision with significant investments in companies like Arm and OpenAI, alongside robotics acquisitions.

title: ICAI CA Foundation Result May 2026: Results to be out on July 3
Pune civic bodies urge community action to prevent water-borne diseases
Chinese scientists develop safer lithium battery that resists extreme heat
Chetan didn't pay, Ketan didn't return: Guard recalls moments before man's murder at Pune fort
description: A security guard's account at Lohagad Fort has shed light on the chilling events leading to Ketan Agarwal's death. He observed Ketan arriving with Siya Goyal, followed by Chetan Chaudhary, who appeared to deliberately conceal his identity. Police allege Siya and Chetan, her lover and co-conspirator, then pushed Ketan into a gorge, with Siya later fabricating a story of an accidental fall. The couple is now in custody.

title: 5 land in jail for drinking alcohol, cooking chicken on Ganga — get bail same day
Mumbai local train murder: How argument over coach door led to stabbing; accused detained
description: Roshan Suvarna, 30, was apprehended in Panvel following a fatal stabbing on a local train. The incident occurred Tuesday night between Andheri and Borivali after an argument with passenger Mayank Lohar over closing a coach door during heavy rain. Lohar sustained a fatal abdominal wound and later died at Shatabdi Hospital. Police identified Suvarna through extensive CCTV analysis.

From his first goal at 17 to 'GOAT' at 39: How Messi built his legacy year by year
description: As Lionel Messi turns 39, his career remains a testament to extraordinary longevity. From scoring his first senior goal at 17 to surpassing 900 career goals, the Argentine has continued to thrive well beyond his prime years. With 351 goals after turning 30, World Cup glory, countless trophies and ongoing success with Inter Miami, Messi has proved that greatness has no age limit.

title: Chiefs, Raiders emerge as top trade destinations for Patriots WR Kayshon Boutte
description: New England Patriots wide receiver Kayshon Boutte continues to generate trade interest, with the Kansas City Chiefs and Las Vegas Raiders identified as potential landing spots. Reports indicate the Patriots are seeking a third-round pick for the 23-year-old, who has one year left on his rookie contract. Despite the speculation, Boutte has publicly expressed his desire to remain in New England, saying he hopes to spend the rest of his NFL career with the Patriots.

Chhattisgarh grocer confesses to killing 8 men in 4 months, tested poison on stray first
description: A grocery store owner in Chhattisgarh has confessed to a chilling serial poisoning spree that claimed eight lives over four months. Ramsahay Jaiswal allegedly used borax powder mixed in liquor to eliminate acquaintances over petty grievances, including loan burdens, perceived black magic, political arguments, and land disputes. He even feigned concern for his victims. The killings were uncovered after a pattern of deaths emerged, leading to his arrest and confession.

BJP claims IAF tender for 36 Rafales exposes Pak propaganda, says 'Rahul owes explanation'
description: The BJP has strongly criticized Rahul Gandhi, accusing him of echoing Pakistan's propaganda regarding the Rafale jets during Operation Sindoor. The party highlighted a recent IAF tender for support for all 36 Rafale jets as proof that Pakistan's claims of shooting them down were false. BJP leaders argue Gandhi questioned the Indian government instead of demanding evidence from Islamabad, thereby legitimizing an adversary's disinformation campaign.

Watch: BMC staffer falls into pothole during Mumbai mayor's flood inspection
description: During Mumbai Mayor Ritu Tawde's inspection of waterlogged Gandhi Market, a civic staffer fell into a channel but was quickly rescued. The incident sparked political criticism, with an MP accusing the mayor of inaction and corruption. Despite the mishap, officials stated the situation was managed. The area, prone to flooding, is now equipped with a recently commissioned ₹19 crore underground pumping station designed to handle heavy rainfall.

Ronaldo vs Messi at FIFA World Cup 2026: CR7 says Portugal vs Argentina clash would be 'top''
description: Cristiano Ronaldo ended a difficult week with two goals as Portugal beat Uzbekistan 5-0, topping Group K at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The 41-year-old became the first player to score in six men's World Cups. After the match, Ronaldo called a potential showdown with Lionel Messi "awesome," a clash that could materialise in the quarterfinals if Portugal secure top spot in their group against Colombia on Saturday.

title: Taylor Swift is reportedly struggling with the Blake Lively fallout
description: Taylor Swift and Blake Lively’s bond has only worsened over the last few months as rumors swirl about the actress being invited to the pop star’s wedding to Travis Kelce which is set to happen in the next few weeks. While Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce focus on their wedding, it seems the pop star has gone through a wave of emotions.

title: After laying off hundreds of engineers, GM swaps 1,000 workers with 50 robots
description: General Motors has introduced 50 collaborative robots at its Michigan Factory Zero, reportedly leading to over 1,000 job eliminations. This move, aimed at cost-cutting and efficiency, sparks criticism from the United Auto Workers union, which views it as a threat to jobs. GM claims the robots support human workers, but the UAW president calls it a "fight for humanity" amid broader automation concerns in the industry.

title: Why your dandruff keeps coming back? Understand science of recurring scalp conditions
description: You wash your hair regularly, and you may switch shampoos and even try home remedies, yet within days, the severe flakes return on your shoulders and come out in chunks. This cycle is what almost every one of us has been through, without even realising why it is happening, even if you are using an anti-dandruff shampoo. This is not a cleanliness problem but a problem with the ingredients of your shampoo and understanding of the science behind dandruff and scalp conditions, so you can finally do something lasting about it.
